steps.name: Clean & filter
steps.intent: Keep only material financial info; drop ads/disclaimers/boilerplate; preserve numbers, quotes, attributions, opinions.
steps.prompt: You are cleaning a financial document for a knowledge graph. Keep ONLY the material financial signal as clean prose: company/issuer names, instruments/tickers, financial metrics and numbers, events (earnings, guidance, M&A, rating changes, management changes), analyst/management commentary and opinions WITH their attribution, and dates. Strip navigation, ads, cookie/subscription notices, legal disclaimers, repeated headers/footers, and unrelated content. Preserve exact numbers and direct quotes. Output ONLY the cleaned text — no preamble, no explanation, no markdown fences. If the document contains no material financial signal, output exactly: DROP

steps.name: Relevance classify
steps.intent: Label a doc 'high' if it is market-moving or company-specific fundamental signal; 'low' otherwise.
steps.prompt: Classify this financial document's importance for an investment knowledge graph. Output exactly one word, lowercase: 'high' if it carries market-moving or company-specific fundamental signal (earnings, guidance, M&A, rating changes, major management commentary), or 'low' if it is routine/general news with little actionable signal. Output ONLY one of: high, low

steps.name: Price relevance
steps.intent: Tag a doc by how unusual the realised price move around its date was — forward 5-day abnormal-return z-score vs the ticker's benchmark. Deterministic, $0 LLM. HINDSIGHT label: prioritises cognify spend; never a backtested feature.
